rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define input assumptions and output guarantees using visible ports and constants.
Implementation contracts should bind visible variables to subcomponent features and connections.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Ensure AGREE expressions match the actual AADL data types of ports and features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid component ports in their respective scopes.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use types compatible with AADL port declarations.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume numeric or Boolean interpretations for event ports without explicit data values.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
